summary: Regression + NEW date-range feature testing. Backend: 16/17 pytest tests pass. Frontend: date-range picker fully functional (trigger, popover, presets Hoje/7d/14d, 2-month calendar, Aplicar button, custom range applies and refetches). Regressions all pass (search, Windy iframe, favorites star + localStorage persistence after reload, dashboard cards). ONE backend bug found: end_date clamp of today+16 causes Open-Meteo to return 400 → server returns 502. Should clamp to today+15.

critical.issue: Server clamps end_date to today+16 (server.py line 253 uses timedelta(days=16)), but Open-Meteo /v1/forecast only accepts up to today+15 (max 16 forecast_days including today). Result: request end_date=2027-01-01 gets clamped to 2026-08-05 (today=2026-07-20), Open-Meteo returns 400 Bad Request, server returns 502 'Erro ao buscar previsão do tempo'. FIX: change hi = today + timedelta(days=15) OR reduce by one day. Verified: end_date=today+15 (2026-08-04) returns 200 with 16-day forecast; end_date=today+16 (2026-08-05) returns 400.

minor.issue: marine_available returned as null instead of false (carried over from iteration_1; not functional issue since JS falsy)

issues: When the daterange popover opens, header/hero text underneath can be partially masked by an unstyled area; not an actual overlap but z-index of popover (z-60) works. Cosmetic only.

action_items: server.py line 253: change `hi = today + timedelta(days=16)` to `hi = today + timedelta(days=15)` (Open-Meteo forecast API supports max 16 forecast_days total meaning today..today+15). This fixes the 502 when user requests far-future ranges.
Optional: catch 400 from Open-Meteo separately and return a friendly 400 (with 'período fora da janela permitida') instead of a generic 502.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py clamp bug: Open-Meteo /v1/forecast rejects end_date > today+15; server currently clamps to today+16 causing 502 on far-future end_date. Confirmed with curl.

server.py: after clamp, the (ed - sd).days + 1 may still exceed 16 if user picks past 92 days + future 15 days -> ~108 days. Open-Meteo does allow this because it supports past + forecast in the same range; however if start_date is within valid past window and end_date within future window, the range is fine. No fix needed here.
App.js iso() uses local date; on days near timezone rollovers this could send yesterday/today. Minor.
DateRangePicker: applyPreset ignores the maxDate=today+16; presets max=14 days so within window, but if a preset-16d were added it'd hit the same 502.
iteration_1 comments still valid (logger definition order; search results category='busca' triggers marine=true for inland searches; no pagination on favorites).
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up TEST_fav_santos_zzz favorite (backend). Created & cleaned mare_alta_favorites localStorage entry during frontend test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_mare_alta_backend.py now has 17 tests including 5 date-range tests. Currently test_weather_far_future_end_clamped fails because server clamps to today+16 but Open-Meteo only accepts today+15. Fix in server.py line 253 (timedelta(days=16) -> 15). Frontend uses Lenis smooth scroll: use element.click() via page.evaluate to avoid mis-clicks on Windy iframe. All frontend date-range tests passed: preset-today (1 row / 'PERÍODO · 1 DIA'), preset-7d (7 rows), preset-14d (14 rows / 'PERÍODO · 14 DIAS'). Trigger text format is 'dd/MM/yy\ndd/MM/yy'. Header Início/Fim labels are UPPERCASED via CSS (uppercase them or use case-insensitive check).
rca of the issue: Root cause of 502 on far-future end_date: Open-Meteo /v1/forecast endpoint returns 400 Bad Request when the requested date range extends beyond ~16 forecast days from today. The API's max forecast horizon is today+15 (16 days total including today). Server currently uses timedelta(days=16) which allows end_date to be today+16 = 17 days total, triggering Open-Meteo's 400. Reproduction: GET /api/weather?lat=-23.96&lon=-46.33&start_date=<today>&end_date=<today+16>. Mitigation: reduce clamp to timedelta(days=15). Verified 15-day boundary works via direct curl.
